name: Ent Credit Union
description_short: Ent Credit Union is a Colorado-based credit union with 60+ years of history offering checking, savings, loans, and business banking through multiple locations including downtown Denver.
description_long:
Ent Credit Union was founded over 60 years ago with the mission of bringing credit services to Colorado residents. Today, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative with multiple service centers across the state, including a prominent location in the historic LoDo district of downtown Denver situated in the 1919 Steelbridge building. The credit union serves a diverse membership including homeowners, professionals, commuters, students, and those new to banking and investing.

Ent offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services including deposit accounts (checking and savings), auto loans, personal loans, business banking products, and mortgage services. Members can access services through multiple channels: in-person at service centers with 24-hour ATM access, online through their website, or via their mobile banking app. The LoDo Service Center specifically provides instant issue Visa debit cards, loan applications, and business account setup, with Member Service Representatives available during lobby hours (9 AM-5:30 PM Monday-Friday).

Ent distinguishes itself through personalized member service, with staff who are locally embedded in their communities. The credit union emphasizes competitive interest rates and a willingness to work with members who may not initially qualify for standard rates, offering guidance on debt consolidation and financial management. Their approach focuses on the whole person rather than credit score alone, with loan officers like Brett Benedetti known for listening to member needs throughout the entire borrowing process. The organization maintains local business banking specialists and employs experienced mortgage professionals.

As a credit union, Ent operates on a member-ownership model rather than shareholder profit maximization, which typically aligns incentives toward member benefits. However, the website content is limited to one service center location, and broader details about membership eligibility, fee structures, and competitive rate information are not provided in the available materials. Members should verify current rates and products on their full website.
address: 4585 N Nevada Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80918

company_info.headquarters: 4585 N Nevada Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80918

pros: Member-owned credit union structure with 60+ year history and established reputation in Colorado
Multiple service channels including online banking, mobile app, and in-person locations with 24-hour ATM access
Personalized loan service with dedicated officers who listen to borrower needs throughout the process
Competitive interest rates in the region with willingness to work with applicants who don't initially qualify
Experienced staff including mortgage specialists, business bankers, and member service representatives
Convenient downtown Denver location in historic building between Cherry Creek and Union Station
No appointment required for opening checking or savings accounts; flexible appointment scheduling for loans
cons: Limited lobby hours (9 AM-5:30 PM weekdays only) may not accommodate all working schedules
Website content does not disclose membership eligibility requirements or specific fee structures
No information provided about specific APR ranges, minimum deposit amounts, or rate comparison data
Appointment recommended for loan applications may delay service compared to some competitors
Limited detail on business loan products, SBA lending, or lines of credit availability
best_for: Colorado residents seeking personalized mortgage or auto loan services with competitive rates
Small business owners in the Denver area needing dedicated business banking relationships
Credit union members prioritizing member-ownership model and community-focused banking over large national banks
diagnosis: Ent Credit Union is best for Colorado residents and Denver-area business owners seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with competitive rates through a member-owned institution. The main caveat is that specific rate information, fee schedules, and membership eligibility requirements are not detailed on the service center webpage, requiring prospective members to contact the credit union directly or visit their main website for complete product details.
services: Financial management advice from Member Service Representatives
Loan payment and fund transfer services
